<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
Household members lack PersonId values. They are identified by name and birthdate instead.

Phase 20 death radiation may require removing the deceased from households. Since HouseholdMember lacks PersonId, this likely means filtering by name and birthdate, marking the household reviewed, or hiding the person from the display.

The plan is to mark the person as deceased, clear their domicile, remove them from the household by name and birthdate, and mark the household reviewed.

RegisterAmendment and BirthDeclaration authorization patterns will be reviewed for reference. A coding agent will handle the large implementation volume.

The domain layer will be written directly. An agent will handle the application, API, and UI scaffolding. Auth, rejection reasons, and birth handlers still need review before writing the domain files.
</details></think>

{
  "description": "Implement Phase 20 death declaration",
  "model": "claude-opus-4-8-thinking-high",
  "name": "general-purpose",
  "prompt": "Implement Phase 20 — Death declaration \u0026 radiation for the SchaerbeekMunicipality .NET solution at `/Users/joba/Projects/Private/dotnet/SchaerbeekMunicipality`.\n\n## Goal\nRegister a death and radiate (remove/mark) a person from the active municipal population register. Follow BirthDeclaration for case machine/UI/dashboard/notifications scaffolding; follow ChangeOfAddress for person-linked Open and confirm-side Person mutations.\n\n## Architecture decisions (do not invent alternatives)\n\n**Aggregate name:** `DeathDeclarationCase` in `Domain/DeathDeclaration/`\n**API:** `/api/death-declarations`\n**UI routes:** `/death-declarations`, `/death-declarations/{id}`\n**Visit reason:** `VisitReason.DeathDeclaration = 5`\n**Document type:** `DocumentType.DeathCertificate = 9` (Death act)\n**Domain event:** `PersonRadiated` (CaseId, PersonId, DeathDate, OccurredAt)\n**Outbound:** TaxAdministration + SocialSecurity (or HealthInsurance if SocialSecurity doesn't exist — check `OutboundNotificationRecipient`) stubs via `OutboundNotification.CreateForDeathDeclaration`\n\n### Checklist flags\n- `PersonIdentified` — set on Open\n- `DeathFactsRecorded` — date, place, informant relationship\n- `DeathActAttached` — supporting document\n- `HouseholdReviewed` — officer marks household reviewed (optional remove from household on confirm)\n\n### Statuses\nSame as Birth: `Intake` → `UnderReview` → `Confirmed` / `Rejected` / `Suspended`\nAuto-transition Intake→UnderReview when death facts recorded.\n\n### Person APIs (add to Person.cs)\n- `DateOnly? DateOfDeath`\n- `bool IsDeceased` (computed or stored — prefer `DateOfDeath is not null` or explicit `IsDeceased` bool; use `DateOfDeath` + clear domicile)\n- `MarkDeceased(DateOnly deathDate)` — sets death date; throws if already deceased\n- `ClearDomicile()` — sets DomicileAddress to null\n- Guard: cannot open death case if already deceased\n\n### Household on confirm\n- Add `RemoveMemberMatching(string givenName, string familyName, DateOnly birthDate)` to `Household` (match case-insensitive name + birth date)\n- Add `FindHouseholdsContainingMemberAsync(...)` or `ListByMemberIdentityAsync` to `IHouseholdRepository` + impl\n- On confirm: mark deceased, clear domicile, remove matching members from any households found, fire PersonRadiated\n\n### Case fields\n- PersonId (required)\n- DeathDate, DeathPlace, DeathAbroad (bool), InformantRelationship (string or enum: Spouse, Child, Parent, Sibling, Other)\n- DeathActDocumentId\n- HouseholdReviewedAt / checklist HouseholdReviewed via `ReviewHousehold()` method\n- Locking fields (Assigned/LockedBy/LockedAt)\n- Decision fields like Birth (Reject/Suspend/Resume)\n- Rejection reasons: MissingDeathAct, PersonAlreadyDeceased, InsufficientEvidence, Other\n\n### Open rules\n- Requires registered person with NR number (like COA)\n- Preferably has active domicile (use domicile or latest registered address like COA — allow open if NR present; prefer requiring domicile OR registered case)\n- Cannot open if `person.IsDeceased` / DateOfDeath set\n- Cannot open if another non-terminal death case already exists for person (optional but good)\n\n## Files to create/modify\n\n### Domain (create folder DeathDeclaration/)\n- DeathDeclarationCase.cs, CaseId, CaseStatus, CaseChecklist, RejectionReason, DeathFacts.cs (VO), InformantRelationship.cs, InvalidDeathDeclarationTransitionException, IDeathDeclarationCaseRepository, PersonRadiatedEventDetails\n- Events/PersonRadiated.cs\n- Update Person.cs, Household.cs, IHouseholdRepository.cs\n- DocumentType += DeathCertificate\n- AdministrativeDocument += DeathDeclarationCaseId FK + CreateForDeathDeclarationCase + BelongsTo...\n- OutboundNotification += DeathDeclarationCaseId + CreateForDeathDeclaration (extend CreatePending)\n- VisitReason += DeathDeclaration\n- VisitReasonLabels update\n\n### Application Features/DeathDeclaration/\nMirror BirthDeclaration slices:\n- DeathDeclarationCaseAuthorization, DeathDeclarationCaseGuard\n- OpenDeathDeclarationCase (Request with PersonId like COA)\n- List, Get (+ DetailDto), Checklist\n- Claim, AutoClaim (if birth has it), ReleaseCaseLock\n- RecordDeathFacts\n- ReviewHousehold\n- AttachDocument, RemoveDocument, DownloadDocument\n- ConfirmRadiation (or ConfirmDeathDeclaration), Reject, Suspend, Resume\n\nRegister all handlers in Application/DependencyInjection.cs\n\nUpdate GetReviewDashboardHandler to include death cases (tiles + actionable queue) — copy birth pattern with ReviewDashboardCaseType.DeathDeclaration\nUpdate ListOutboundNotificationsHandler for DeathDeclaration case type\nPerson file GetPersonFile / DTO: expose IsDeceased / DateOfDeath\n\n### Infrastructure\n- Persistence/Configurations/DeathDeclarationCaseConfiguration.cs → table `death_declaration_cases`\n- Update AdministrativeDocumentConfiguration, OutboundNotificationConfiguration, PersonConfiguration for new fields\n- Repositories/DeathDeclarationCaseRepository.cs\n- Extend HouseholdRepository\n- Events: IPersonRadiatedHandler, PersonRadiatedNotificationHandler, DomainEventDispatcher switch case\n- PersonFileQuery: include death cases in ListCasesByPersonIdAsync, history, documents; filter deceased from household display of OTHER people's files when member is deceased; show Deceased in person status\n- MunicipalDbContext DbSet\n- DependencyInjection registrations\n- EF migration: `dotnet ef migrations add Phase20DeathDeclaration --project src/SchaerbeekMunicipality.Infrastructure --startup-project src/SchaerbeekMunicipality.Api` (from repo root)\n\n### API\n- Features/DeathDeclaration/* endpoints + DeathDeclarationEndpoints.cs\n- Map in MunicipalApiHostExtensions\n\n### Web\n- Api/DeathDeclaration/IDeathDeclarationApi.cs + DeathDeclarationApiClient.cs — register in DI where Birth client is registered\n- Features/DeathDeclaration/Pages: CaseList + CaseDetail (two-column: person, death facts, document, household review | officer decision)\n- Components as needed (document panel, decision panel)\n- Reception: NewCaseDialog + NewRegistrationCasePage — DeathDeclaration visit reason → resolve person (like COA) → open death case → navigate `/death-declarations/{id}`\n- ReviewDashboard: death tiles + navigation\n- MainLayout nav item\n- Person file: Deceased status chip; hide/disable COA and document request actions when deceased; show death case on Cases tab\n- MunicipalDocumentDownloadProxy if needed for death docs\n\n### Tests\n- Domain.Tests/DeathDeclaration/DeathDeclarationCaseTests.cs — cannot confirm without document; cannot open/mark already-deceased; happy confirm clears domicile conceptually via Person tests\n- Domain.Tests for Person.MarkDeceased\n- Integration.Tests/Features/DeathDeclaration/ — happy path confirm updates person+household; unauthorized reject/confirm; review dashboard includes death cases\n- Follow existing test helper patterns from BirthDeclarationTestHelpers / ChangeOfAddressTests\n\n### Docs\nUpdate `docs/phases/phase-20-death-declaration-radiation.md` status to Complete with deliverables checklist like phase-12\nUpdate `docs/phases/README.md` and `docs/ROADMAP.md` status for phase 20\n\n## Implementation style\n- Match existing code style exactly (file-scoped namespaces, primary constructors for handlers, strongly typed IDs)\n- Do NOT add unnecessary comments\n- Do NOT create README files\n- Prefer copying Birth/COA files and adapting over inventing new patterns\n- After coding, run `dotnet build` and `dotnet test --filter \"FullyQualifiedName~DeathDeclaration\"` (and Person deceased tests) and fix failures\n- Also run a broader test filter if quick enough to ensure you didn't break Birth/COA/PersonFile\n\n## Key reference files\n- Domain/BirthDeclaration/BirthDeclarationCase.cs\n- Application/Features/ChangeOfAddress/OpenChangeOfAddressCase/OpenChangeOfAddressCaseHandler.cs\n- Application/Features/ChangeOfAddress/ConfirmAddressChange/ConfirmAddressChangeHandler.cs\n- Application/Features/BirthDeclaration/* (all slices)\n- Api/Features/BirthDeclaration/BirthDeclarationEndpoints.cs\n- Web/Features/BirthDeclaration/Pages/*\n- Infrastructure/Events/DomainEventDispatcher.cs\n- Application/Features/Registration/GetReviewDashboard/GetReviewDashboardHandler.cs\n\nReturn a summary of what was implemented, any deviations, and test results.",

}

_**Agent (default)**_

**Phase 20 is in place** — death declaration & radiation, end to end. Docs mark it Complete.

### What you get
- **`DeathDeclarationCase`** with checklist (person, death facts, death act, household review), locking, confirm/reject/suspend
- **Confirm radiation** marks the person deceased, clears domicile, removes them from matching households, fires `PersonRadiated` → tax + social-security notification stubs
- **API** `/api/death-declarations/*` and **UI** `/death-declarations` (+ detail)
- **Reception** `VisitReason.DeathDeclaration` → NR lookup → open case
- **Review dashboard** tiles (Death unassigned / Radiations ready)
- **Person file** deceased chip; amendment hidden when deceased
- Open guards on COA, document request, and register amendment for deceased persons

### Demo path
1. Reception → Death declaration → pick registered resident  
2. Record death facts + attach death act + review household → confirm  
3. Person file shows deceased; domicile cleared; notification log has stubs  

Tests covering this: **8 domain + 5 integration** for death (broader related suite also green).
